cves.name: Heartbleed TLS Heartbeat Memory Disclosure
cves.description: The TLS heartbeat extension in OpenSSL 1.0.1 through 1.0.1f allows remote attackers to obtain sensitive information (private keys, passwords, session data) from process memory via crafted packets.
cves.recommendation: Upgrade OpenSSL to >= 1.0.1g. Revoke and reissue all TLS certificates.

cves.name: DROWN Cross-Protocol Attack on TLS via SSLv2
cves.description: An attacker can decrypt TLS sessions by exploiting SSLv2 support. If any server sharing the same RSA key pair supports SSLv2, all servers using that key are vulnerable.
cves.recommendation: Disable SSLv2 on all servers. Upgrade OpenSSL to >= 1.0.2g or >= 1.0.1s.

cves.name: CCS Injection (ChangeCipherSpec) MITM
cves.description: OpenSSL before 0.9.8za, 1.0.0m, and 1.0.1h does not properly restrict processing of ChangeCipherSpec messages, allowing MITM attackers to hijack sessions.

cves.name: Padding Oracle in AES-NI CBC MAC Check
cves.description: An attacker can use a padding oracle attack against an AES CBC ciphersuite when the connection uses an AES CBC cipher and the server support AES-NI.

cves.name: POODLE SSLv3 Padding Oracle
cves.description: The SSL protocol 3.0 uses nondeterministic CBC padding, allowing a man-in-the-middle attacker to obtain cleartext data via a padding-oracle attack (POODLE).
cves.recommendation: Disable SSLv3 entirely. Use TLS 1.2+ only.

cves.name: X.509 Email Address 4-byte Buffer Overflow
cves.description: A buffer overrun can be triggered in X.509 certificate verification, specifically in name constraint checking. An attacker can craft a malicious email address in a certificate to overflow four bytes on the stack.

cves.name: X.509 Email Address Variable-Length Buffer Overflow
cves.description: A buffer overrun in X.509 certificate verification name constraint checking. An attacker can use a crafted email address to overflow an arbitrary number of bytes containing the '.' character on the stack.

cves.name: X.400 Address Type Confusion in X.509 GeneralName
cves.description: Type confusion when processing X.400 addresses inside an X.509 GeneralName. When CRL checking is enabled, an attacker can pass arbitrary pointers to a memcmp call, enabling memory disclosure or DoS.

cves.name: BN_mod_sqrt Infinite Loop DoS
cves.description: The BN_mod_sqrt() function contains a bug causing it to loop forever for non-prime moduli. Attackers can trigger this via crafted certificates for denial of service.

cves.name: c_rehash Command Injection
cves.description: The c_rehash script does not properly sanitise shell metacharacters, allowing command injection when processing specially crafted file names in certificate directories.
cves.recommendation: Upgrade OpenSSL to >= 1.0.2ze, 1.1.1o, or 3.0.3. Use 'openssl rehash' instead of c_rehash.

cves.name: SM2 Decryption Buffer Overflow
cves.description: A bug in the implementation of the SM2 decryption code could lead to a buffer overflow. An attacker who can present SM2 content for decryption can overflow the buffer by up to 62 bytes.

cves.name: ASN.1 String Read Buffer Overrun
cves.description: Read buffer overrun when processing ASN.1 strings in the X509_aux_print function. An attacker can crash OpenSSL or read adjacent memory, potentially disclosing sensitive information.

cves.name: Use-After-Free Following BIO_new_NDEF
cves.description: Use-after-free following BIO_new_NDEF. If an application constructs an ASN1 output stream and BIO_new_NDEF is called, the stream is not properly cleaned up, enabling a use-after-free.

cves.name: SSL_select_next_proto Buffer Overread
cves.description: Calling the OpenSSL API function SSL_select_next_proto with an empty supported client protocols buffer can cause a crash or memory contents to be returned to the caller.

cves.name: DH Small Subgroups Key Recovery
cves.description: OpenSSL generates X9.42 style DH parameters with a short private key length, making it possible for an attacker to recover the private key via a small subgroup attack.

cves.name: GENERAL_NAME_cmp NULL Pointer Dereference
cves.description: The X.509 GeneralName type is a generic type for representing different types of names. The GENERAL_NAME_cmp function mishandles certain name types, leading to a NULL pointer dereference and crash.

cves.name: Alternative Chains Certificate Forgery
cves.description: During certificate verification, OpenSSL will attempt to find an alternative certificate chain. An attacker could use this to forge RSA certificates.

cves.name: PKCS12 NULL Pointer Dereference DoS
cves.description: Processing a maliciously formatted PKCS12 file may cause OpenSSL to crash leading to a denial of service. Applications that load PKCS12 files from untrusted sources are affected.
